_doc: Selection-tuning corpus for the prompt → chart-type gate. Each case is a realistic plain-English diagram request + the chart-type id(s) any correct `suggest_chart_type` top-1 may land on (membership in `accept` counts). Seeded from dgmo/tests/fixtures/prompt-suite.json (36) + the 10 inline cases mirrored from suggest.test.ts = 46. `accept` ids must exist in @diagrammo/dgmo chartTypes. `accept` is ORDERED: `accept[0]` is the CANONICAL/preferred answer (use it when one type is clearly best; leave order arbitrary for genuinely ambiguous unions). The hard gate counts membership in `accept`; a separate ADVISORY 'primary hit-rate' (non-gated, printed by the test) tracks how often the scorer lands `accept[0]` — quality signal that membership can mask. `baseline` = the top-1 pass-count the regression gate ratchets against (raise it as the harness fixes cases; never lower without re-baselining). `dgmoVersion` records the @diagrammo/dgmo the baseline was measured against — scores can shift when dgmo bumps. A case may carry `"wontfix": true` (+ optional `"note"`) to PARK it — a prompt we've decided not to solve as worded (too ambiguous / good-enough); parked cases are excluded from the active pass-count + baseline but kept here as documented known-limitations. Grow this aggressively via the harness's add-a-case control.
